name: Southwestern Steak Salad
recipeIngredient: 3/4 pounds flank steak, cut diagonally against the grain into 1/4-inch thick strips
1 head romaine lettuce, washed, dried and torn into pieces (about 3 to 4 cups)
1/2 red bell pepper, seeded and diced
Lime wedges and cilantro sprigs for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small food processor or blender combine the lime juice, ground cumin, cayenne, garlic, jalapeno (if using), and 1 tablespoon vegetable oil. Puree until smooth.
2. Place the flank steak in a shallow dish and pour half of the vinaigrette over it. Toss to coat well. Let marinate for 15 minutes.
3. Reserve the remaining vinaigrette.
4. Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over moderately high heat until hot but not smoking. Stir-fry the steak for 2 minutes, or just until pink. Transfer to a bowl.
5. In a large bowl combine the lettuce, corn, red onion, red bell pepper, and cilantro. Whisk the reserved vinaigrette (if necessary) and pour over the salad. Toss to coat.
6. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Divide the salad between two plates and top with the cooked beef in a decorative pattern. Garnish with lime wedges and cilantro sprigs.
Yield: 2 servings
